topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that seemed to snag in my throat. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in over a decade—a familiar, sneering grin. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally tapped to view the request. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. *No. Absolutely not.* I quickly minimized the window, the silence of my room suddenly oppressive. I paced three steps to the window, my gaze fixed on the street below, wishing the pavement would swallow me whole. Every nerve ending felt hyper-alert, waiting for the inevitable next move from that familiar shadow.

[story 2]

I stared at the phone, the screen illuminating my face with a harsh, unforgiving light. The name staring back was a physical blow. My hands started to sweat, slick against the cool glass. A frantic internal monologue began, a rapid-fire sequence of 'what ifs' and 'how do I delete this?' My jaw locked so tight I could feel the muscles strain in my neck. I backed away from my desk, bumping into a bookshelf, the sudden movement jarring. I pressed my palms against my eyes, trying to filter the image out, but the image seemed to burn behind my lids. The air in the room felt thick, heavy, as if I couldn't draw a full, steady breath.

[story 3]

The sound of the incoming request felt amplified, echoing in the stillness of my apartment. I sat frozen on the edge of the sofa, my limbs feeling strangely heavy and disconnected. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm that drowned out the ambient noise of the city. I kept glancing at the screen, my pupils wide, drinking in every detail of the profile. A wave of intense internal pressure washed over me, making it difficult to focus on anything else. I stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing distance, but my legs felt unsteady, as if wading through thick water. I retreated to the hallway, leaning against the wall, trying to force my breathing into a slow, controlled rhythm.
